meaning: n.来处,根源adv.从何处,从哪里 conj.拒此 pron.何处
examples: No one ordered him back whence he came.
The straw began and said: "Dear friends, from whence do you come here?"
The peasants were astonished, and said, "Peasant, from whence do you come?"
If you know whence you came, there is really no limit to where you can go.
It would behoove our present generations to remember from whence the wealth we enjoy came.
